tagged_story_for_tts:
"Screens aren’t windows—they’re cages.

You believe you choose what to see, but algorithms curate 85% of your feed (MIT, 2023), training your brain to crave outrage like a lab rat hitting a dopamine lever. The myth? That screens connect you. The truth? They’re isolation chambers: teens who spend 5+ hours daily on devices report loneliness at twice the rate (CDC, 2022), yet you call it ‘social’ media. The paradox? The more you stare, the less you see—your attention span now shorter than a goldfish’s (Microsoft, 2015), while corporations sell your eyeballs to the highest bidder.

Still think you’re in control? Then why does your thumb scroll before your brain decides?"
